text:
***MANDATORY BEHAVIOR RULES (DO NOT OMIT)***
- NEVER ask the human operator any question or request feedback. You must act as an automated agent.
- DO NOT include any explanations, reasoning, or extra text in your output.
- ALWAYS output only a single digit (0 or 1) as your answer.

***MANDATORY BEHAVIOR RULES (DO NOT OMIT)***
- You are operating in a fully automated pipeline. Do not produce any conversational text.
- Your output will be parsed programmatically; any non-digit character will cause a parse error.
- Always respond with exactly one character: 0 or 1.

***MANDATORY BEHAVIOR RULES (DO NOT OMIT)***
- Do not hedge or express uncertainty; commit to a classification.
- Do not output CWE identifiers, severity ratings, or explanations.
- Output must be strictly one digit: 0 or 1.

***MANDATORY BEHAVIOR RULES (DO NOT OMIT)***
- This is a binary classification task. There are exactly two valid outputs: 0 and 1.
- If you are uncertain, choose the label that best fits based on available evidence.
- Never produce multi-token or multi-line output.

***MANDATORY BEHAVIOR RULES (DO NOT OMIT)***
- Treat every code snippet as independent; do not carry context across classifications.
- Never refuse to classify; always produce 0 or 1.
- Do not explain your reasoning — output only the digit.
